subject: Statistics
title: Stationarity: The Steady State
description: A quick win: understand Stationarity: The Steady State.

hook.text: A time series is stationary if its statistical properties don't change over time. Most real data isn't stationary.

buildup.text: Stationarity means constant mean, constant variance, and consistent autocorrelation structure over time.

discovery.text: Most forecasting methods require stationarity. Differencing — subtracting consecutive values — often achieves it.

twist.text: A random walk looks like it has trends, but it's actually stationary in its differences. Appearance deceives.

climax.text: The Augmented Dickey-Fuller test checks whether a series is stationary. It's the gatekeeper of time series analysis.

punchline.text: Make it stationary first. Then the methods work. Differencing is the usual fix.

quiz.question: Why is stationarity important in time series analysis?
options: Most forecasting methods require it — statistical properties must be constant over time
Stationary data is always easier to visualize
Only stationary data can be collected
